meta.subtitle: Plain-English market read · Gap-risk check · Long-weekend after a hawkish Fed

verdict.label: YELLOW · Trade small or sit out
verdict.desc: Yesterday the new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh delivered a hawkish surprise — a dot plot showing many officials want a rate HIKE this year — and stocks fell about 1.2% while bond yields jumped. Today the market is digesting that shock. The bigger problem: markets are closed Friday for Juneteenth, and the Iran peace deal gets signed Friday in Geneva while you can't trade. Any spread you sell today is exposed over a 3-day weekend with live headline risk. Take one small trade at most, or simply wait until Monday.

composite.band_note: MILD · up from 44 · post-Fed shock + 3-day weekend

gap.band_note: Below the danger line · but it covers a 3-day weekend with the Iran signing

focus.jbig: Digesting a hawkish Fed · 3-day weekend ahead
focus.jtag: Warsh's dot plot flipped from cuts to possible hikes — yields jumped
focus.jnote: Today is a "wait it out" day. Yesterday's Fed was the big shock — a very hawkish dot plot showed roughly half of officials want at least one rate hike this year, the opposite of the cuts markets expected entering 2026. Stocks dropped about 1.2% and the 10-year yield jumped to ~4.50%. Now you face a 3-day weekend: markets are closed Friday for Juneteenth, and the Iran peace deal is set to be signed Friday in Geneva with the Strait of Hormuz reopening — all while you can't trade. Everything you sell today rides through that. The smart move is to wait until Monday.

notdown: Energy and defensives held up best relative to the tape, but there's no fresh bullish catalyst — and shorting puts into a hawkish-Fed, rising-yield backdrop is low grade. Wait.
notup: Rate-sensitive growth & tech (NVDA, MSFT, META, mega-cap) is the cleaner read down: higher-for-longer rates pressure long-duration stocks, and Wed's losses were led by mega-cap tech. But it's pre-holiday with the Iran signing looming, so size down hard.
why: Why YELLOW and not RED? The raw numbers (48 and 30%) are still inside the calm zone, so this isn't a full stand-down. But two rules apply: never carry full size over a long weekend, and respect a fresh policy shock. The Fed just flipped from cuts to possible hikes and the Iran deal signs Friday while the market is closed. Take one small, well-supported trade if it checks out at 9:35 AM, or just wait. A cleaner read returns Monday 6/22 once the weekend headlines are priced in.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~16.4, still low but up ~12% on the Fed
Big events (next 1–3 days) — 3-day weekend + Iran signing Friday while closed
World risk — Iran deal not final; Trump warned he could "go back to dropping bombs"
Volatility trend — ticking up after the hawkish dot plot
Commodities — oil at 3-month lows (~$76), calming for inflation

snapshot.delta: rose ~12% Wed · still low but rising

setups.read: Higher-for-longer rates pressure long-duration tech, and these led Wed's drop. But shorting into a fast move right before a 3-day weekend with the Iran signing is risky. Half size at most.
Oil is at 3-month lows and Hormuz reopens Friday, which caps the group — but the signing happens over the closed weekend, so headlines could whipsaw oil Monday. Stand down into the holiday.
Small caps lag on rising yields, but that's a macro drift, not a single named catalyst. Not a clean Sniper setup. Wait.
Relative safety in a risk-off tape, but rising yields hurt rate-proxy utilities and there's no fresh catalyst or built support. Low grade.
Brand-new IPO (public June 12, ~$208): no options history, no support levels, parabolic post-IPO move. Can't use it.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Quiet, choppy digestion, then the long weekend
scenarios.sub: Market absorbs the hawkish Fed; light pre-holiday volume; everyone flat into Friday's close

scenarios.label: Bad case — Yields keep rising, tech keeps sliding
scenarios.sub: Hike fears stick → more pressure on long-duration growth and small caps

scenarios.label: Good case — Oversold bounce, dip-buyers step in
scenarios.sub: Stocks stabilize as oil at 3-month lows eases the inflation story → relief rally

scenarios.label: Surprise — Iran signing stumbles over the weekend
scenarios.sub: Geneva ceremony slips or Trump's "back to bombs" warning escalates → oil & vol gap Monday

risks: 3-day weekend gap risk · markets CLOSED Fri 6/19 (Juneteenth) — any spread you hold rides through Friday and the weekend with no ability to trade.
Iran deal signs Friday in Geneva while the market is closed — Trump said Hormuz "completely reopens" Friday but also warned he could "go right back to dropping bombs"; oil could gap Monday.
Hawkish dot plot · cuts flipped to possible hikes — roughly half of officials penciled in at least one 2026 hike; growth and tech can keep dropping if yields rise further.
10-year yield near one-year highs (~4.50%) — rising rates keep pressuring long-duration tech and small caps.
Warsh dropped forward guidance — the Fed said it "can't give guidance on what's next," raising policy uncertainty and the odds of bigger surprises ahead.

rows.today: Broad risk-off: SPX −1.21%, tech led down

rows.delta: hold fire over the long weekend
